title: Modality (Μ)
description: What could be - alternatives, rejected options, possible futures

alternatives.option: Partial scope (canonical + data only, like US-001)
alternatives.reason: US-001 demonstrated this leaves features incomplete

browser-vs-static-validation.decision: Accept static validation when browser testing blocked

alternatives.option: Block story completion until browser testing works
alternatives.reason: Environmental issue shouldn't block completion when code is correct
Chosen - sufficient evidence of implementation correctness

generic-crud-mode-terminology.option: Use generic CRUD terminology (create/update/createOrUpdate) for import modes
generic-crud-mode-terminology.rejectedBecause: Violates canonical DSL purity - MoneyWorks uses insert/update/replace
generic-crud-mode-terminology.currentApproach: Use exact MoneyWorks REST API mode names: insert, update, replace
generic-crud-mode-terminology.tradeoff: Less familiar to developers expecting CRUD terms, but maintains DSL consistency
generic-crud-mode-terminology.context: IMPT-001 D-002: Chose MoneyWorks terminology over generic CRUD

automatic-controller-registration.option: Automatically register all controllers via file scanning
automatic-controller-registration.rejectedBecause: Explicit registration provides clear control and visibility
automatic-controller-registration.tradeoff: Manual step required, but clear and auditable
automatic-controller-registration.context: After US-001 missed registration, considered automatic approach but rejected

detail-prefixed-field-names.option: Use MoneyWorks API field names directly with 'Detail.' prefix in interface
detail-prefixed-field-names.rejectedBecause: Creates inconsistent DX - Detail.Account vs Account in other entities
detail-prefixed-field-names.currentApproach: Strip 'Detail.' prefix in postProcess(), restore in prepare()
detail-prefixed-field-names.tradeoff: Extra processing step, but cleaner interface for developers
detail-prefixed-field-names.context: TXNS-001 D-001: Decided to strip prefix for cleaner DX

composite-key-for-contacts.option: Use ParentSeq + Order as composite primary key for Contacts
composite-key-for-contacts.rejectedBecause: SequenceNumber is MoneyWorks canonical primary key, already unique
composite-key-for-contacts.tradeoff: Requires separate lookup for contacts by parent, but matches MW design
composite-key-for-contacts.context: CONT-001 D-001: Decided to follow MW table design rather than create synthetic key

16-bit-only-roles.rejectedBecause: Would break GetContactForRole compatibility with Contact1/Contact2 legacy fields
16-bit-only-roles.tradeoff: Adds complexity to role enum, but enables full MW compatibility
16-bit-only-roles.context: CONT-001 D-002: Extended beyond 16-bit for backward compatibility

radix-over-native-html.option: Use Radix UI primitives for chat UI components
radix-over-native-html.rejectedBecause: React 18/19 type conflicts in monorepo cause Radix components to show type errors
radix-over-native-html.currentApproach: Use native HTML elements (button, input, scroll div) with Tailwind styling
radix-over-native-html.tradeoff: Less abstraction and accessibility built-in, but avoids type system issues
radix-over-native-html.context: AIPOC-001 D-001: Native HTML chosen over Radix due to type conflicts

json-in-response-vs-code-blocks.option: Return artifact JSON inline within AI response text
json-in-response-vs-code-blocks.rejectedBecause: Inline JSON mixes with conversational text, harder to parse reliably
json-in-response-vs-code-blocks.currentApproach: Use fenced ```artifacts code blocks for structured artifact data
json-in-response-vs-code-blocks.tradeoff: Requires regex parsing, but provides clean separation of text and data
json-in-response-vs-code-blocks.context: ARTF-001 D-001: Code block format chosen for artifact data transport

full-pl-sections-vs-simplified-summary.option: Include full P&L sections in daily transaction summary
full-pl-sections-vs-simplified-summary.rejectedBecause: Sample MoneyWorks report shows simplified transaction type format, not full P&L
full-pl-sections-vs-simplified-summary.currentApproach: Use simplified format: Type, Gross, GST, Nett, Count matching MW canonical report
full-pl-sections-vs-simplified-summary.tradeoff: Less detail, but aligns with MoneyWorks report structure and keeps focused
full-pl-sections-vs-simplified-summary.context: DTXN-001 D-001: Chose simplified data model to match MoneyWorks Daily Transaction Summary format

complete-moneyworks-entity-coverage.future: All 20+ MoneyWorks entities implemented with full-stack pattern

product-controller-completion.blockers: None - just needs registration in TableRegistry

ai-package-tool-use.future: COMPLETED - Extend packages/ai with Anthropic Agent SDK tool use capabilities
ai-package-tool-use.outcome: AI assistant can read/write files, execute bash, search codebase via 6 tools

ai-skills-for-reports.future: Add Anthropic Skills API for generating polished Excel/PDF reports from MoneyWorks data
dependencies: AITOOLS-001 provides data fetching via tools ✅
enabledBy: Skills generate formatted documents in sandbox
Hybrid architecture: tools for data, skills for presentation
ai-skills-for-reports.outcome: AI generates Executive Summary XLSX/PDF reports from live MoneyWorks data

ai-moneyworks-query-tools.future: Add MoneyWorks-specific tools: query_accounts, get_transactions, run_report
dependencies: Authentication handling for MW API calls
ai-moneyworks-query-tools.outcome: AI can directly query MoneyWorks data, analyze accounts, generate financial insights
